summary: Review of post-external-feedback improvements to the NetworkPolicy sub-reconciler (CC-0039). Changes address three items from sourcery-ai[bot]: (1) condition type/reason strings extracted into typed constants, (2) defensive fail-closed guard for empty ingress sources when CRD validation is bypassed, (3) explicit merge strategy documentation on ensureNetworkPolicy. All 20 NetworkPolicy tests pass, go vet is clean, and the implementation correctly follows the established sub-reconciler patterns.

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases are covered (brownfield-only, managed-db, managed-cache, both-managed, empty ingress, additional egress, no-change skip)
Error cases tested (ensure error propagated, delete error propagated)
Assertions specific (exact values, not just no error)
Empty ingress security guard tested with fail-closed verification

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing sub-reconciler patterns (HPA three-path lifecycle)
DECISION comments explain non-obvious choices (DNS scope, package locality)

security_checklist.item: Empty ingress guard fails closed (refuses to create allow-all NetworkPolicy)
CEL XValidation on NetworkPolicySpec enforces size(self.ingress) > 0
SetControllerReference called on both create and update paths
RBAC marker synced with Helm ClusterRole template

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
NetworkPolicyReady in subConditionTypes after HPAReady before BootstrapReady
reconcileNetworkPolicy called after reconcileHPA, before reconcileBootstrap
ensureNetworkPolicy kept in controller package (DECISION: no second consumer yet)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: npNormalizeMap is minimal utility needed for snapshot comparison

fail_fast_checklist.item: Empty ingress detected before NetworkPolicy construction
Errors propagated immediately with descriptive wrapping

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated (ingress sources at runtime, not just CRD)
IgnoreNotFound on delete path prevents errors on already-deleted resources
Snapshot comparison skips no-op updates to avoid API churn

suggested_improvements: Consider extracting condition type/reason constants in sibling sub-reconcilers (reconcile_hpa.go, reconcile_deployment.go, reconcile_database.go, reconcile_secrets.go, reconcile_bootstrap.go) to match the pattern established here. All currently use inline string literals. This is a cross-cutting improvement tracked by review pattern extract-repeated-condition-strings-into-typed-constants.md — not a requirement for this PR.
